code: @Manual{,   title = {forestIPM: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics},   author = {Willian Vieira},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.1},   url = {https://github.com/willvieira/forestIPM}, }

what: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
text: forestIPM R package implementing Bayesian hierarchical Integral Projection Model (IPM) designed study tree population dynamics eastern North America. combines growth, survival, recruitment models, parameterized function climate competition using forest inventory data. package provides two engines compute population growth rates (λ\lambda) 31 tree species simulate plot-level community dynamics time. framework enables users explore demographic processes scale community patterns. broadly, forestIPM can used investigate effects climate warming tree performance, evaluate sensitivity λ\lambda climate competition (📄), examine stochastic λ\lambda influences species range limits (📄), provides foundation studying mechanisms driving species coexistence, many .

title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
text: complete description methodology, ranging fitting hierarchical Bayesian demographic models constructing IPM applying ecological questions, available companion book: 📓 Forest Demography IPM Book

title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
text: package website provides function reference. workflow structured around 5 constructor functions used define key components model: stand() representing forest plot species_model() defining species model env_condition() specifying climate drivers parameters() defining single reproducible parameter realization Bayesian posteriors control() Configure IPM projection settings components passed two main IPM engines: lambda() computing population growth rate (lambda) per species project() projecting population community dynamics time step--step introduction package, see Get Started vignette IPM Book.

title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("willvieira/forestIPM")

title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics
text: mathematical model underlying forestIPM formally verified using Lean 4 Mathlib. component model pipeline corresponding Lean module proves key mathematical properties — convergence, non-negativity, boundedness — hold construction, independent numerical implementation. tests R code; machine-checked proofs mathematical model internally consistent. source files live lean/. Table auto-generated lean/proof_status.sh. “Sorry” indicates theorems stated yet proved (open proof obligations).

title: Bayesian Hierarchical Integral Projection Model for Tree Population Dynamics

what: Configure IPM lambda and project engine settings — control
title: Configure IPM lambda and project engine settings — control
text: Configure IPM lambda project engine settings

title: Configure IPM lambda and project engine settings — control
code: control(   delta_time = 1,   years = 100,   store_every = 1,   compute_lambda = TRUE,   progress = TRUE,   integration_method = "gauss-legendre",   n_gl = 200L,   bin_width = 1 )

title: Configure IPM lambda and project engine settings — control
text: delta_time Positive numeric. Duration timestep years. Default 1. years Positive integer. Number simulation timesteps. Used project() engine. Default 100. store_every Positive integer. Store stand state every N timesteps. Used project() engine. Default 1. compute_lambda Logical. Whether compute asymptotic lambda timestep. Used project() engine. Set FALSE skip (faster projections population structure needed). Default TRUE. progress Logical. Whether display progress bar projection. Used project() engine. Default TRUE. integration_method Character. Integration method kernel discretization. One "midpoint" (uniform midpoint rule) "gauss-legendre" (Default; Gauss-Legendre quadrature, higher accuracy). n_gl Positive integer. Number Gauss-Legendre nodes integration_method = "gauss-legendre". Ignored "midpoint". Default 200. bin_width Positive integer. Bin width IPM kernel discretization integration_method = "midpoint". Ignored "gauss-legendre".. Default 1.

title: Configure IPM lambda and project engine settings — control
text: object S3 class "ipm_control".

title: Configure IPM lambda and project engine settings — control
code: ctrl <- control(years = 10, compute_lambda = FALSE, progress = FALSE) print(ctrl) #> <ipm_control>  10 years | dt=1.0 | store_every=1 | bin_width=1 | lambda=no | progress=no | integration=gauss-legendre

what: Compute Gauss-Legendre quadrature nodes and weights on [a, b] — .gl_nodes_weights
title: Compute Gauss-Legendre quadrature nodes and weights on [a, b] — .gl_nodes_weights
text: Uses Golub-Welsch algorithm: constructs symmetric tridiagonal Jacobi matrix Legendre polynomials [-1, 1], decomposes via getJacobiEigen() (RcppEigen SelfAdjointEigenSolver), maps result interval [, b].

title: Compute Gauss-Legendre quadrature nodes and weights on [a, b] — .gl_nodes_weights
code: .gl_nodes_weights(n, a, b)

title: Compute Gauss-Legendre quadrature nodes and weights on [a, b] — .gl_nodes_weights
text: n Positive integer. Number quadrature nodes. Numeric. Left endpoint integration interval. b Numeric. Right endpoint integration interval.

title: Compute Gauss-Legendre quadrature nodes and weights on [a, b] — .gl_nodes_weights
text: named list two numeric vectors: nodes Quadrature nodes [, b], sorted ascending. weights Corresponding quadrature weights (positive, sum b - ).

what: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
text: Compute asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species

title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
code: lambda(mod, pars, stand, env, ctrl = NULL)

title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
text: mod ipm_spModel object. pars ipm_parameters object. Must contain parameters least one species mod. Lambda computed species present mod pars - species stand used competitors appear output. stand ipm_stand object. Provides size distributions species (focal competitors). env ipm_env object. Climate drivers. ctrl ipm_control object NULL. bin_width delta_time used. NULL, defaults used.

title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
text: object S3 class "ipm_lambda" - named numeric vector one element per focal species (species available parameters).

title: Compute the asymptotic population growth rate (lambda) per species — lambda
code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = c(150, 200, 350),                  species_id = "ABIBAL",                  plot_size  = 1000) s    <- stand(df) mod  <- species_model(s) pars <- parameters(mod, draw = "mean") env  <- env_condition(MAT = 8, MAP = 1200) ctrl <- control(years = 5, compute_lambda = TRUE, progress = FALSE) lam  <- lambda(mod, pars, s, env, ctrl) print(lam) #> <ipm_lambda>  draw=mean #>   ABIBAL: 2.0763

title: Build the IPM Kernel — mkKernel
text: Assembles full Integral Projection Model kernel (K = P + F) focal species given size distribution vectors environmental conditions.

title: Build the IPM Kernel — mkKernel
code: mkKernel(   Nvec_intra,   Nvec_inter,   delta_time,   plotSize,   Temp,   Prec,   pars,   plot_random )

title: Build the IPM Kernel — mkKernel
text: Nvec_intra List. Intraspecific size distribution. Nvec_inter List. Interspecific size distribution. delta_time Numeric. Time step years. plotSize Numeric. Plot area square meters. Temp Numeric. Mean annual temperature (degrees Celsius). Prec Numeric. Mean annual precipitation (mm). pars Named list. Species-specific parameters elements growth, mort, rec, sizeIngrowth. plot_random Numeric vector length 3. Plot-level random effects growth (1), mortality (2), recruitment (3).

title: Build the IPM Kernel — mkKernel
text: named list elements K (full kernel), P (growth x survival kernel), F (recruitment kernel), square matrix dimension equal number mesh points.

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
text: Plot ipm_projection object

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
code: # S3 method for class 'ipm_projection' plot(x, type = NULL, timestep = NULL, ...)

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
text: x ipm_projection object returned project. type Character NULL. One "lambda", "pop_size", "size_dist", "lambda_vs_n". NULL (default), four figures rendered sequence. timestep Integer NULL. used type = "size_dist". stored timestep (year) plot. NULL (default), last stored timestep used. Must one values x$years. ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
text: x, invisibly.

title: Plot an ipm_projection object — plot.ipm_projection
code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = seq(130, 600, by = 50),                  species_id = "ABIBAL", plot_size = 400) s    <- stand(df) mod  <- species_model(s) pars <- parameters(mod, draw = "mean") env  <- env_condition(MAT = 8, MAP = 1200) ctrl <- control(years = 5, compute_lambda = TRUE, progress = FALSE) proj <- project(mod, pars, s, env, ctrl) plot(proj, type = "lambda")  plot(proj, type = "size_dist", timestep = 3)

what: Project population or community dynamics through time — project
title: Project population or community dynamics through time — project
text: Project population community dynamics time

title: Project population or community dynamics through time — project
code: project(mod, pars, stand, env, ctrl)

title: Project population or community dynamics through time — project
text: mod ipm_spModel object. pars ipm_parameters object. stand ipm_stand object. Initial stand state (t = 0). env ipm_env object. Climate drivers (may time-varying). ctrl ipm_control object. Required.

title: Project population or community dynamics through time — project
text: object S3 class "ipm_projection" fields: $species, $years, $lambda, $stand_series (list ipm_dist_snapshot objects, holding continuous size distribution per species timestep), $summary.

title: Project population or community dynamics through time — project
code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = c(150, 200, 350),                  species_id = "ABIBAL",                  plot_size  = 1000) s    <- stand(df) mod  <- species_model(s) pars <- parameters(mod, draw = "mean") env  <- env_condition(MAT = 8, MAP = 1200) ctrl <- control(years = 5, compute_lambda = TRUE, progress = FALSE) proj <- project(mod, pars, s, env, ctrl) print(proj) #> <ipm_projection>  1 species | 5 years | store_every=1 | draw=mean

Used values numeric vector; ignored values named list. NULL values numeric vector, applies species.

code: df <- data.frame(size_mm = c(150, 200, 350),                  species_id = "ABIBAL",                  plot_size  = 1000) s     <- stand(df) mod   <- species_model(s) pars  <- parameters(mod, draw = "mean") pars2 <- set_random_effects(pars, values = c(0, 0, 0))

title: forestIPM 1.0.1
text: New integration_method argument control() — choose "midpoint" "gauss-legendre" (default) quadrature IPM kernel discretization. GL quadrature achieves higher accuracy fewer mesh points smooth integrands. New n_gl argument control() — sets number Gauss-Legendre quadrature nodes (default 100). GL quadrature uses Golub-Welsch algorithm (eigendecomposition symmetric tridiagonal Jacobi matrix) compute nodes weights — external dependencies, implemented base R eigen().

title: forestIPM 1.0.1
text: mkKernel() now uses per-column weight vectors (weights * kernel_column) instead uniform h * matrix(...) scaling. midpoint integration result identical; GL quadrature non-uniform weights applied correctly. Replaced base R eigen() C++ Eigen::SelfAdjointEigenSolver via RcppEigen Golub-Welsch eigendecomposition — faster node/weight computation large n_gl values. ipm_lambda ipm_projection objects now store full stand, env, pars, ctrl objects conditions attribute instead individual scalar fields — enables complete reproducibility saved result. print() summary() ipm_lambda ipm_projection now correctly display time-varying climate inputs "function(t)" env$MAT env$MAP function. Added Lean 4 formal mathematical proofs core IPM components: Gauss-Legendre quadrature, midpoint rule, growth/survival/ingrowth vital rates, kernel assembly, asymptotic lambda, competition, climate scaling, community dynamics.

title: forestIPM 1.0.1
text: Fixed lambda(): building size distributions now uses focal species instead union species stand — single-species models longer include extraneous species mesh computation (#BUG-04). Fixed project() on_missing = "static": static competitors now correctly freeze initial sizes throughout projection instead incorrectly dropped (#BUG-05). Removed null-guard parameters() silently returned empty parameter lists species data found — parameters() now raises informative error immediately (#BUG-06).

title: forestIPM 1.0.1
text: Added tests set_random_effects() covering draw type resolution, seed reproducibility, random effect application across species. Added integration test two-species interactions, verifying interspecific competition affects lambda expected.

title: forestIPM 1.0.0
text: Initial release forestIPM — Bayesian hierarchical Integral Projection Model (IPM) tree population dynamics eastern North America, covering 31 species across 3 demographic model variants.

title: forestIPM 1.0.0
text: New composable API five typed S3 constructors: stand() — initialise forest stand data frame trees (size mm DBH, ≥ 127 mm threshold) species_model() — select species demographic model variant fuzzy-match suggestion typos parameters() — load vital-rate parameters draw ("mean", "random", integer index) optional seed env_condition() — supply climate inputs (MAT/MAP) numeric scalars time-varying function(t) control() — configure simulation settings (years, delta_time, store_every, bin_width) Two IPM engines: lambda() — computes asymptotic population growth rate (dominant eigenvalue) via C++ RcppEigen solver project() — runs timestep projection loop returns full ipm_projection object lambda trajectories, stand series, summary tibble supported_species() — returns tibble 33 eastern North America tree species common names, French names, model availability plot.ipm_projection() — three-panel visualisation via type argument dispatch: "lambda", "size_dist", "lambda_vs_n", NULL panels S3 print summary methods six classes: ipm_stand, ipm_spModel, ipm_parameters, ipm_env, ipm_control, ipm_projection

title: forestIPM 1.0.0
text: Replaced sequential outer() loops vectorised rep(meshpts, times = n) / rep(meshpts, = n) expansion Replaced truncnorm::dtruncnorm() equivalent dnorm() / pnorm() formula using base R, eliminating hotspot consumed 46% inclusive profiling time 100-year projection runs reduced ~76 s ~54 s Removed truncnorm package dependency (longer needed optimisation)

title: forestIPM 1.0.0
text: Fixed getEigenValues() (C++ via RcppEigen): replaced Eigen::SelfAdjointEigenSolver (symmetric-) Eigen::EigenSolver returning .real() component — IPM kernels non-negative non-symmetric, symmetric solver produced incorrect eigenvalues (#BUG-01) Fixed init_pop(): removed exists('fct') global environment check replaced local fct <- 1 initialisation -loop, preventing silent failures function ran non-global scopes (#BUG-02) Fixed getPars_sp() pars_to_list(): replaced deprecated purrr::as_vector() unlist(use.names = TRUE), preserving named-vector contracts used downstream parameter indexing (#BUG-03)
